papers.title_en: SCOReD: Student-Aware CoT Optimization for Recommendation Distillation
papers.authors: Haz Sameen Shahgir, Yufei Li, Frank Shyu, Luke Simon, Sandeep Pandey, Xi Liu, Yue Dong
papers.evidence: 用于蒸馏的思维链轨迹优化
papers.tldr: 针对推荐系统中大模型思维链（CoT）蒸馏存在的冗余、推理不确定性及分布偏移问题，本文提出SCOReD框架。该框架通过解析教师模型的推理轨迹，利用学生模型的注意力机制评估各片段重要性，并动态执行保留、重写、融合或剪枝操作。实验表明，SCOReD在提升推荐性能（NDCG和Recall）的同时，显著缩短了推理长度，为学生模型提供了更高效的学习信号。
papers.abstract_en: Chain-of-thought (CoT) distillation in the recommendation domain is a necessary precursor to RL training, but raw teacher traces are ill-suited to this task. Large teachers approach the recommendation task with unusually high reasoning uncertainty, repeatedly rechecking their answers without revising them; supervised fine-tuning on such traces produces verbose students that never revise their initial guess. Furthermore, due to the novelty of the recommendation domain, the teacher's reasoning traces are highly out-of-distribution for the small student LLM.   We propose Student-Aware CoT Optimization for Recommendation Distillation (SCOReD), a CoT optimization framework tailored to recommendation that first parses each teacher trace into typed segments and uses the student LLM's attention to score the importance of each segment. Then SCOReD dynamically selects a per-segment edit (KEEP / REWRITE / FUSE / PRUNE) based on the output length and comparative log probability lift of the answer given the edit as per the student. Therefore, SCOReD prunes redundant sections of the reasoning trace while preserving information-dense sections and adapts raw teacher traces to the student's output distribution. Training on SCOReD-optimized CoTs provides a cleaner learning signal to the student model and improves over baseline SFT by 1.56% NDCG and 1.9% Recall@5, while reducing reasoning length by 27.3%.

papers.title_en: ReCoLoRA: Spectrum-Aware Recursive Consolidation for Continual LLM Fine-Tuning
papers.evidence: 大语言模型持续适配的参数高效微调
papers.tldr: 针对大语言模型在持续微调中容易发生灾难性遗忘的问题，本文提出ReCoLoRA框架。该方法通过频谱感知的递归整合机制，在每个新任务开始前将当前有效权重重新分解为冻结残差、主成分和新适配器，使模型能持续吸收前序任务知识。在多个7-8B模型上的实验表明，ReCoLoRA以更少的参数实现了优于LoRA、PiSSA等基线的性能。
papers.abstract_en: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning adapts a large language model to one task cheaply, but across a task sequence LoRA-style methods keep stacking low-rank updates on the same frozen weight, so each new task tends to overwrite the previous ones. We present ReCoLoRA (Recursive Consolidation of Low-Rank Adapters), a spectrum-aware framework for continual fine-tuning: adapters are initialized from a randomized SVD of the pretrained weight, per-layer effective ranks are selected by an elbow criterion, and the principal subspace is adapted before residual capacity is opened. Before each new task, ReCoLoRA re-decomposes the current effective weight, rather than the original one, into a frozen residual, a slowly updated principal component, and a fresh adapter (recursive consolidation), so every task starts from the model that has already absorbed its predecessors. On a six-task continual GLUE sequence over four 7-8B backbones, ReCoLoRA attains the best final average score on three of the four backbones against rank-swept LoRA, PiSSA, AdaLoRA, and DoRA baselines while training fewer parameters; an oracle-routed task-bank variant serves as an upper bound under full task isolation. Code: https://github.com/bhqy666/ReCoLoRA.

papers.title_en: Switch-Reasoner: Learn When to Think in Multitask Mixtures via Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Yiyang Fang, Pei Fu, Jinjie Li, Jian Liang, Wenke Huang, Ruijie Luo, Shaojie Zhang, Jian Luan, Yi R. Fung, Mang Ye
papers.evidence: 通过强化学习自适应选择推理模式
papers.tldr: 针对多模态大模型在混合任务中固定推理模式导致的效率低下问题，本文提出 Switch-Reasoner 框架。该框架基于 GRPO 强化学习，将“思考”视为虚拟工具调用，使模型能根据输入难度自主选择直接回答或显式推理。通过双层调节机制解决训练不稳定性，在保持高性能的同时显著减少了不必要的计算开销，实现了更优的精度与效率平衡。
papers.abstract_en: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) often follow a fixed Think-then-Answer paradigm, which is inefficient in heterogeneous multitask settings because simple inputs may not require explicit reasoning while difficult ones can benefit substantially from it. Learning when to think is also unstable during post-training, where imbalanced rollouts can drive the model toward always-thinking or always-direct behavior. We propose Switch-Reasoner, a GRPO-based framework that learns to adaptively select reasoning modes for MLLMs. It treats thinking as a virtual tool invocation and allows the model to either answer directly or invoke explicit reasoning before answering. To stabilize this decision, we introduce a dual-level regulation mechanism that balances the overall use of Thinking Mode and Direct Mode while providing sample-level supervision based on the relative benefit of the two choices. Experiments on 11 multimodal tasks show that Switch-Reasoner reduces unnecessary reasoning while maintaining strong performance, achieving a better accuracy-efficiency trade-off.

papers.title_en: MASTE: A Multi-Agent Pipeline for Zero-Shot Aspect Sentiment Triplet Extraction
papers.authors: Ao Hong, Lehang Wang, Zhirun Yue, Mingxin Wang, Zihan Wang, Houde Liu
papers.evidence: 思维链提示与零样本部署
papers.tldr: 针对大语言模型在零样本方面情感三元组抽取（ASTE）中因单次生成负担过重而表现不佳的问题，本文提出了MASTE框架。该框架采用多智能体流水线，将复杂的ASTE任务分解为四个顺序阶段，由专门的智能体分别处理子任务。MASTE无需训练且不依赖标注数据，在多个基准测试中显著优于现有的零样本和思维链基准，有效缩小了与监督学习方法的性能差距。
papers.abstract_en: Aspect Sentiment Triplet Extraction (ASTE) requires jointly identifying (aspect, opinion, sentiment) triples from a given review sentence. While large language models (LLMs) achieve strong zero-shot performance on many NLP benchmarks, their effectiveness on ASTE remains limited, as single-pass generation forces the model to determine span boundaries, opinion grouping, and sentiment polarity in a single decoding step. Common remedies, such as few-shot in-context learning and chain-of-thought prompting, offer only marginal improvements and rely heavily on either in-domain demonstrations sampled from labeled training data or carefully engineered reasoning prompts, neither of which is broadly available in zero-shot deployment. Inspired by the classical agent paradigm, we propose MASTE, a multi-agent pipeline for zero-shot Aspect Sentiment Triplet Extraction. MASTE decomposes ASTE into four sequential stages, where specialized agents handle different compositional subtasks with explicit conditioning on prior outputs. This design enables entirely training-free zero-shot ASTE and generalizes across different backbones and datasets. Extensive experiments on four ASTE benchmarks show that MASTE substantially outperforms zero-shot and chain-of-thought LLM baselines under the same backbone, narrowing the gap to fully supervised methods without using any labeled triplets. Code is available at https://github.com/Hankerlove/MASTE.
